articles.title: Spirit Electronics Announces Managed Access to U.S.-Based Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing for Aerospace and Defense Programs
articles.summary:
PHOENIX, AZ — June 09, 2026 — Spirit Electronics today announced a new managed-access offering that provides aerospace and defense customers with a secure pathway to advanced, U.S.-based semiconductor manufacturing in […]

articles.title: Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite
articles.summary:
Apolink has made contact with its first satellite after launching on SpaceX’s July 7 rideshare mission, clearing the way for a data relay demonstration using a novel experimental license from the Federal Communications Commission.
The post Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
articles.summary: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of about 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center’s Greenbelt, Maryland, campus Tuesday to the adjoining Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The property, formerly known as NASA Goddard’s Area 400, is now part of the largest block of unfragmented forest between […]

articles.title: Arianespace opens door to GEO rideshare opportunities from 2029
articles.summary:
MILAN — Arianespace has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with French in-orbit servicing company Infinite Orbits to explore future collaboration on multiple direct-to-GEO rideshare launch services, the companies announced July […]
The post Arianespace opens door to GEO rideshare opportunities from 2029 appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
articles.summary: Following its longest hibernation period ever of nearly a year, NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft has emerged in good health and is ready to begin transmitting science data gathered in the distant Kuiper Belt far beyond Pluto. On June 23, flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, confirmed New Horizons, […]

articles.title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
articles.summary: In this photograph, the Artemis II crew participates in the dedication of the Apollo 14 Moon tree at the Lunar Receiving Park at NASA’s Johnson Space Center on June 25, 2026. This tree is a second-generation Apollo Moon tree of the loblolly pine species. The original Apollo Moon trees were grown from seeds carried aboard […]

articles.summary: Your challenge is to tell us the location of the satellite image and why it is interesting.

articles.title: SpaceX launches Transporter-17 amid concerns about rideshare program’s future
articles.summary:
SpaceX launched the latest in its Transporter series of rideshare missions July 7 as industry concerns about the program’s future reach what one rival company executive called a panic.
The post SpaceX launches Transporter-17 amid concerns about rideshare program’s future appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Isar Aerospace Signs US$112.5M Deal for Dedicated Launch Site in Canada
articles.summary:
German rocket builder Isar Aerospace has signed an agreement with Canada’s Maritime Launch Services to develop a dedicated launch facility at Spaceport Nova Scotia. The first launch of an Isar Aerospace Spectrum rocket from the site is expected in 2028. Announced on 7 July, the agreement will see Maritime Launch Services provide a licensed launch […]
The post Isar Aerospace Signs US$112.5M Deal for Dedicated Launch Site in Canada appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation
articles.summary:
Belgium’s Simera Sense has won a contract to provide multispectral imagers for a small constellation being designed to track wildfires, volcanic activity and other regional priorities for the Canary Islands off the coast of Spain.
The post Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site
articles.summary:
Isar Aerospace has signed a contract to develop a Canadian launch site for its Spectrum rocket, an agreement linked to a deal to sell submarines to the Canadian military.
The post Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site appeared first on SpaceNews.
